records.ety: [LL. intimidatus, p. p. of intimidare to frighten; pref. in- in + timidus fearful, timid: cf. F. intimider. See Timid.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Intimidated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Intimidating (?).]

defs: To make timid or fearful; to inspire of affect with fear; to deter, as by threats; to dishearten; to abash.
quotes: Now guilt, once harbored in the conscious breast, Intimidates the brave, degrades the great.
